title: Jellyflow Node Drag Kernel v1
problem: Jellyflow needs a renderer-neutral node drag kernel so Rust adapters can get XyFlow-like node dragging through headless runtime transactions and harness fixtures instead of renderer-local reimplementations.

evidence.summary: Opened the node drag kernel workstream from the interaction harness follow-on, with ADR 0003 renderer-free testing and ADR 0002 schema-boundary constraints.

Added public runtime::drag single-node planning/apply helpers and harness-backed fixtures covering successful SetNodePos commit traces plus missing, hidden, non-draggable, no-op, non-finite, and global-disabled no-commit cases.
Extended drag planning to co-drag selected nodes with the primary node, expose ordered NodeDragItem output, filter non-draggable and selected-parent child nodes, and commit sorted SetNodePos ops.
Added shared snap-offset planning, global extent group clamping, per-node rect extent clamping, and node-origin-aware bounds math for renderer-neutral node drag.
Added renderer-neutral node drag gesture payloads, interaction harness tracing, XyFlow-compatible drag callbacks, adapter conformance coverage, and public surface smoke coverage.

continue_policy.notes: Close the node drag kernel lane by documenting strategy, rerunning closeout gates, and splitting follow-ons for parent expansion, auto-pan, public fixtures, or renderer smoke tests.
